column1: Sentence
SCANNER OF FILES ON RESOURCES WITH KNOWN PASSWORDS
Technical Specifications
IDEA DESCRIPTION
On entry we have an access list of the form
ssh://user:pass@host:port
ftp://user:pass@host:port
We need to filter only those servers that have files matching the mask.
SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS
The program is designed as a module in accordance with the documents module_HOWTO and
"code and assembly design".
Project name: fscan
The module sends the following events with the fscan tag:
- "Version build %DATE% %TIME%" (once at startup)
- "File found: %filename% at %user%@%server%:%port%" - when a file is found by mask
- "Results sent to server" - when the found matches are sent successfully
- "No result detected, give up" - if the module is finished running and nothing is found (hypothetical case).
In this case, the module should issue the WantRelease event (see "module_HOWTO") to unload from memory
CONFIGS
The name of the config is the Ctl argument of the Control function, the contents of the config is the CtlArg argument (see modules_HOWTO.txt)
Configs must be in any single-byte encoding (preferably ASCII).
The module accepts a single fsrv config containing a list of control server addresses,
separated by \r\n or \n, in address:port format.
If the port is even, the operation is HTTP, if odd - HTTPS.
If a protocol prefix is specified (http/https), the prefix takes precedence over the specified port.
The module works with the control server that was reached first, for each request.
WORKING WITH THE COMMAND SERVER
The module communicates with the command server via HTTP(s) protocol with simple requests.
The number of scanning threads:
GET /<group>/<clientid>/fscan/th HTTP/1.1
The group and clientid values are struct ParentInfo fields
CHAR ParentID[256];
CHAR ParentGroup[64];
(see module_HOWTO)
The response is a non-negative number.
If atoi(response) == 0, then the default number of threads = std::thread_concurrency() - 1.
The scanner gets the list to scan by HTTP request to the server
GET /<group>/<clientid>/fscan/domains HTTP/1.1
Response format:
proto://user:pass@host[:port][\r]\n
(one or many entries)
The scanner retrieves a list of regular expressions to search for with the following request
GET /<group>/<clientid>/fscan/rules HTTP/1.1
Response format:
regexp1[\r]\n
regexp2[\r]\n
(one or many entries)
When scanning, the scanner searches for ANY file that matches the regularization.
After finding a file, scanning continues.
The scanner gets the starting directory for the search with the request
GET /<group>/<clientid>/fscan/home HTTP/1.1
The format of the response is:
path1[\r]\n
path2[\r]\n
(one or many entries)
These are the directories to be searched.
If there are several directories, you should search in each of them.
If the answer is empty, search the current directory.
The scanner gets the search depth by requesting
GET /<group>/<clientid>/fscan/nest HTTP/1.1
Response format:
A non-negative integer less than 100.
If the response does not fit this rule, the default depth is 10.
The number 0 means there is no depth limit.
When we finish searching through the given list, we let the server know about it:
GET /<group>/<clientid>/fscan/over HTTP/1.1
The server's response is the same as to the /domains request - a new list of domains to work with.
In case of an unexpected response (empty list, error code, etc.)
the module goes to idle (scanning is stopped).
and makes the same request once every 10 minutes (time is a constant).
The results are sent via the DPOST protocol (see "DPOST password robbing TOR" for a description of the protocol) using the following request
POST /<group>/<clientid>/fscan/81
HTTP/1.1
The collected data is sent in a multipart/form-data container with source and data fields.
The value of the source field is "Files found"
Data field value: plain text, line separator \r\n
Record format:
proto://user:pass@host:port/path/to/file\r\n
(one or many entries)
You can get the frequency of sending dummy data from the control server by HTTP request
GET /<group>/<clientid>/fscan/freq HTTP/1.1
In the body of the response we expect a number - this is the number of seconds, not more often than which the data should be sent.
If it is 0 - we send it as soon as a new result is ready.
If it is a positive number - we accumulate records in the buffer and send once in X seconds,
clearing the buffer on successful sending.
REGULAR EXPRESSIONS
Uses its own regular expression syntax.
An expression can contain asterisk, question mark and square brackets "[]".
* Asterisk denotes any sequence of characters, including an empty one.
A question mark indicates any single character.
[] Square brackets list a list of single characters, for example,
[jeu] - it can only be replaced by one of the three characters "j" , "e" or "u",
[?] - only the question mark character,
[[] - only the opening square bracket,
[[*] - asterisk symbol only,
[[*] - asterisk or opening square bracket.
Пример: q[abc]erty[?]u[[]uu??88]88*
qaerty?u[uuzz88]88444 - fits.
qbertysu[uuzz88]88444 - not suitable
qcerty?u[uuz88]88 - does not fit.
qaerty?u[uuz?88]88 - fits.
qrerty?u[uuz?88]88 - does not fit.
qaerty?u!uuz?88]8811 - does not fit.
